title: Was Kaworu really the only one that had feelings for Shinji
display_title: Was Kaworu really the only one that had feelings for Shinji
wikitext:
[]: Was Kaworu really the only one that had feelings for Shinji?             Quote Post by Dataprime » Wed Oct 23, 2013 9:03 pm I see Misato as more of a motherly type figure Asuka never really stated her true feelings for him of course they are hints in the show (but you never know) That leaves me with Rei...I don't know what to think about Rei so I'll put her aside for now. So let us speculate!

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Oct 23, 2013 9:07 pm Kaworu didn't actually have any feelings for Shinji. He's a thing. Someone grew him in a lab. He doesn't even identify himself as human. We see how long and how hard Rei has to struggle to come to terms with the human part of her being. There's something decidedly off about Kaworu's demeanor and attitudes. He's a honeypot. He doesn't love Shinji, he barely knows him. All that he represents is an idyllic but totally hollow fantasy that Shinji must crush before he can [s:237xcw70]answer the Riddle of Steel[/s:237xcw70] [s:237xcw70]cross the abyss[/s:237xcw70] experience and reject Instrumentality. No, Shinji, a gorgeous, nonthreatening confidante is not going to drop into your lap and declare undying love without any change, effort or growth on your part.

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Wed Oct 23, 2013 10:14 pm Chuck, you sound almost jelly. That depends on what kind of feelings you're speaking of - Obviously Misato has some motherly affections for Shinji, but if you mean attraction as in the sexual kind, I'm not seeing much there but Tears kind of plays with that a little and so does EoE. Asuka, it's hard to say for me. She seems like she might "like" Shinji because he's: 1) A fellow pilot so one of the few people "worthy" of her affections 2) Does better than her later on in the show and she's jealous 3) She demands attention from everyone, and Shinji wasn't giving her too much of it so she's peeved I would say Rei does have feelings for Shinji, but she's pretty new to this whole "emotion" thing so I don't know if what you would identify as "like" comes into the equation. She might just feel less lonely when she's with Shinji and so she wants to be close with him. How close? I don't know. But Tears makes it pretty clear that he eases her loneliness. Kaworu... I dont even. I have no idea. He's just weird to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Madonna » Wed Oct 23, 2013 10:54 pm Yes. It's never shown nor alluded that angels can't experience feelings anywhere in the anime, Kaworu did have feelings for him; the issue on whether these feelings were of a sexual or platonic nature can definitely be debated and argued however the fact remains that Kaworu thought he loved Shinji and expressed that urge. Even if it was established that angels couldn't experience any feelings whatsoever, Kaworu was presented as being in a human male form, he had an apparent gender; Kaworu is Adam and as Gendo excitedly stated in episode eight Adam was "The first human"; making Kaworu more of a human and angel hybrid than anything else. To call Kaworu's feelings as him just being 'naive' and him 'not knowing what feelings are' is to not give his character and his emotions any importance or credence whatsoever. I also think he was the only one that had any true meaningful feelings for him, not to belittle what Asuka and others may have felt for Shinji, but they weren't really explored so we'll never really know (Although Asuka's felt more like a high school crush than anything).

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Oct 23, 2013 11:15 pm Kaworu is physiologically partially human. It stands to reason that he's capable of emotion. What I question is that whether his experiences as a living being could grant him any emotional development, or whether the time in which he knew Shinji would allow for any depth or genuineness of feeling. On both of those points, I think the answer is "no" and it's more likely that he was throwing around terms like "love" without knowing what they actually mean. He's vitally important- but as an obstacle, not a friend, confidante, or love interest. Like all of the angels Shinji has to fight, he represents a test. In this case the battle is primarily one of will. Shinji doesn't really kill Kaworu, he kills the part of himself that's capable of accepting this non-threatening being showing up to offer him unconditional love when everyone else is harsh and demanding. I mean in a symbolic sense. He still splats the little bastard.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Oct 24, 2013 12:37 am Chuckman wrote: He's a thing. Someone grew him in a lab.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Oct 24, 2013 2:43 am Feelings for Shinji? Gendo - generally wishes him well but doesn't want to engage with him in any way. Misato - genuine love and affection, but she isn't always sure how to show that appropriately. Asuka (original) - wants him to like her, otherwise just (usually) friendly coworkers/rivals. Asuka (DC cut) - she likes him all right, but hates herself for having feelings for such a chump. Rei 2 - will do anything to keep him safe, up to possibly even ignoring an order. Rei 3 - Shinji is the only person who matters, and genuinely wanted to become one being with him. Kaworu - more interested in Shinji as an avatar for mankind than as a person, but would bone. Toji and Kensuke - he's a good dude, always means well. Hikari - If Toji wasn't around she'd probably be one of Shinji's fan club girls. Kaji - part-time dad/cool older bro.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Oct 24, 2013 5:03 am I don't get what exactly the OP means by feelings : romantic feelings? If it's that, then no, that's not the feelings Kaworu had for Shinji, to quote a term from TvTropes, Kaworu follows a "Blue and Orange morality" : he doesn't follow the same set of rules and morales than mankind, because mentally he's not an human, but an Angel(well, technically a FAR, but he existed for far more long as ADAM than a FAR), and physically speaking he was born only a few days before episode 24. Kaworu follows his own rules : remember that he was very curious and even fascinated about the Lilin and what their civilization achieved(with a preference for music), he still didn't had a problem with obliterating all Lilith-based life by reuniting with his old body, ADAM. It's only after staying some time along the Lilin, and especially Shinji whom he saw his capacity to get back up and continue even though he suffers greatly as something really admirable(which is darkly ironic since it's Kaworu's suicide by Eva which made Shinji reach his breaking point), and when he realized that ADAM wasn't even in Terminal Dogma but Lilith(meaning that SEELE tricked them) that he decided that actually he would prefers that the Lilin stays the inhabitant of Earth instead of taking back his planet and fill it with ADAM-based lifeforms. When he said to Shinji that he loves him, that was not in an "I want to bone you" way, but in a platonic and "pure" way, like Jesus loved everyone. Shinji showed signs of sexual attraction because he's a Lilin and at this point of the story confused about how he felt toward the people around him(with Rei "dead", Asuka in a catatonic state after a mental breakdown and Misato transformed into a cold avatar of vengeance) and also desperate for some affection(which doesn't undermine Kaworu's "love" for Shinji) Of course since Kaworu doesn't have any idea of Lilin's etiquette when interacting with one each other, it looks like he's really trying to get in Shinji's pants, like with the hot bath they shared. But in the end such "pureness" is not human, and Shinji has to deal with he fact that such a perfect, immediate and unconditional love doesn't exist, except maybe with your parents, but since Yui is inside a giant cyborg and Gendo being... well Gendo, Shinji must learn to make it in life without them.

[]:             Quote Post by Literary Eagle » Thu Oct 24, 2013 11:06 am Kaworu likes the song "Ode to Joy" and uses it as an example of what he admires so much about the Lilin. Looking at an English translation of the lyrics reveals that it's about brotherhood and friendship. It also contains the line "a friend loyal unto death", which explains why Kaworu made the final decision that he did. I think the reason why Kaworu seems suspicious to so many fans is because he's just so open about his feelings, which is not "natural" to most people, especially in a show full of characters who are afraid to get too close because of the Hedgehog's Dilemma. But Kaworu's not faking it, he just lacks the social conditioning that causes folks to be more reserved about expressing such sentiments. So, do I believe that Kaworu loved Shinji? Yes, absolutely. I don't know whether it was platonic or romantic in nature, and I don't particularly care. It was beautiful either way. But do I believe that Kaworu was the only one who had feelings for Shinji? Nope. Rei defied orders to eject from Unit-00, blowing herself up to save Shinji. Rei , defying orders? Enough said. And as for Asuka, I think she had conflicted thoughts about him, but liking him was part of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Oct 24, 2013 11:56 am The first conception of Rei as discribed in the proposal(to be found in this very wiki) already discribed her as "learned about feelings once she fell in "love" with someone." Of course, not everything that was in the proposal made it into the original (Such as the Ruins of Arka, Fuyutsuki being Gendo's junior rather than his senior, Ritsuko having a thing for punk-rock), but what we I wouldn't necessarily quote the sacrifice itself as biggest telltale mark here - You can care about someone without necessarily having the hots for 'em. The accompaining talk of "becoming one", Armisael's twisted mockery of a lustful embrace which prompts her to realize this in the first place, the prevalance of typical teenage blushing, and the asessment by various other characters, however, give us Keep in mind that they're 14 - Of course they're not going beyond little acts of affection and clueless stuttering yet. Doubting the Kaworu thing, however, is complete bogus with all those statements about him being supposed to be "impossible to dislike" - and Anno chalking any dislike of him up to insufficient writing on his own part, and Prano even having him referred to as "same-sex love interest" [with the implications] With Asuka, it becomes a matter of definitions - Was the mutual urge to boink and typical love interest writing conventions/narrative styles present? Yes. On a pilosophic/relationsip psychology level, was "true love" where you're together on an equal level, take the other person as they really are and want to be with the other for their own sake? Nope. It's hormones. Miyamura has clearly answered the question to whether Asuka "really likes" Shinji with "No", in case her reactions whenever he was in danger or about to leave the city weren*'t obvious enough, and while I wouldn't take her accussations in EoE completely at face value, her complaints about the honesty of Shinji's own feelings weren't entirely without a few grains of truth - Realistically seen, they were basically being average teenagers, though. So they could be said to "love" each other in the sense that you once "loved" that Ex you're always complaining aboput - the word may very well be appropiate for many contexts, such as the technical/functional/narrative structure ones.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Oct 24, 2013 12:04 pm Unrelated, but Kendrix, why are so many of your phrases stop mid-sentence?

[]:             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Thu Oct 24, 2013 12:16 pm Sexually? I don't think so, just makes good fanservice. There wasn't THAT much screentime to make an actual relationship and it makes more sense that Kaworu's love is platonic cause he is Adam similar to Rei being Lilith. That doesn't stop the staff from pairing them up Anno included. The drafts were like the earliest KaworuXShinji fics before the show was even made.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Oct 25, 2013 8:36 am ElMariachi wrote: Unrelated, but Kendrix, why are so many of your phrases stop mid-sentence?

[]:             Quote Post by Th3Marauder » Fri Oct 25, 2013 10:22 am The way I see it, Kaworu "loves" Shinji a god would love his creations. Which of course is strange because Shinji is technically responsible for the deaths of Kaworu's actual creations.

[]:             Quote Post by EVAfacepalm » Fri Oct 25, 2013 11:53 pm Th3Marauder wrote: The way I see it, Kaworu "loves" Shinji a god would love his creations. Which of course is strange because Shinji is technically responsible for the deaths of Kaworu's actual creations.

[]:             Quote Post by Malevolo » Sat Oct 26, 2013 1:03 am EVAfacepalm wrote: Kaworu gives the impression he does to the viewers because he is exactly what Shinji wants in terms of expression of feelings.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Oct 26, 2013 12:33 pm Nah, you might be getting too close to hamville here. That a good thing isn't permanent, or that you may not be able to keep it, doesn't necessarily have to take away from its inherent level of goodness. It's the unignorable circumstances that create the problem, the surroundings - The point of the exercise is not to shame people for daring to do things that they like and make them feel good. There's only something problematic about it when it occurs at the cost of something. It's not like Kaworu doesn't profit from Shinji in some ways - he gets his curiosity satisfied, for example, he gets to live new experiences and be special for someone. Shinji himself has this huge inferiority complex towards Kaworu, but that may not necessarily be, and probably isn't what Kaworu thinks or what his ideal outcome/state for the relationship is. Kaworu probably thought he was the one's who's disproportionally fortunate to meet this kindred spirit. We shouldn't get caught up in Shinji's perception just because he's the PoV character - Quite tellingly, he was the one who insisted on lying on the floor when he came to visit Kaworu. He did somewhat recognize this at the end, that they were similar ("Somehow he was like Rei... and like me... I loved him as well. ") but not how far - they were both in a situation where they had to make a huge choice for all of their kind, even thought they were curious and even sympathetic towards the other side, for example. His visit to Rei, at least, does partially indicate that he was deliberately seeking out individuals he percieved as "like him." Like all of us. Most notably, he couldn't self-terminate either. Even Rebuild!Kaworu, who very much can (This one is "older and wiser" and seems to have made his decision a long time ago; Maybe witnessing the suffering and loss of humans after NTI convinced him), appears to percieve the whole Fourth-Impact situation like he's the one who got Shinji into needless trouble through a personal miscalculation (he expected the LoC to be there), instead of the other way around, and merely sees his following sacrifice as cleaning up the mess for Shinji's sake - what this clearly is, however, perhaps more obviously than in the original version, where Kaworu had a theoretical gain from causing TI himself, is a "more hero than thou" moment; One of them had to die, and Kaworu was the one who had the streght to do it, which, in either version, only streghtens Shinji's complex of "unworthyness" and his seeing Kaworu as existing on some sort of pedestral, like his bed back in the sleepover scene. it's not like Kaworu (and similar things could be said about Rei - So, Shinji gets her to open up - but not just to him, Rebuild is more pronounced here in that she starts saying hello to people, and has these "Thank you" scenes with Asuka or even Mari, but the original series also had these scenes with her and Touji. but she also pushes him , prompting Touji to muse on his hidden nice guy qualities ) simply takes Shinji as he is... and done with it. He does show him acceptance, but he also talks about Shinji's way of shutting himself off, for example. At least in Kaworu's case, we do have official confirmation that he was also partially meant as "the person who tries to start resolving Shinji's complexes", allthough being the source of one obviously made that harder... it kind of kept Shinji from making this connection that his life, and the rest of the world are now something Kaworu gave him and should be treated as such. That kind of "lone survivor mentality" isn't the solution to everything, Misato's and Kaji's cases make that all too clear, but it may have given him something to go on untill he found something else, so he could do the things that had to be done in the meantime... and there was plenty of shit to do in episode 25'.

[]:             Quote Post by Literary Eagle » Sat Oct 26, 2013 5:06 pm Well said, Kendrix! Shinji and Kaworu's unfortunate circumstances didn't mean that the feelings involved were fake or wrong. I think that dismissing Kaworu's love as a falsehood that had to be destroyed is to unfairly ignore the significance of his sacrifice. Being "a friend loyal unto death" (as described in the "Ode to Joy" lyrics) is an ultimate act of love and friendship... that would be a pretty contradictory way to say "Oh, by the way, this kind of love is fake". I've often seen people dismiss Kaworu and Shinji's interactions as "not real" or "unhealthy" because Shinji allegedly just gets all of Kaworu's love and devotion without having to do anything in return. But I don't think that's really fair to Shinji. He's actually quite kind to people like Rei and Kaworu who are perceived by others as odd or even downright creepy. Look at how Misato (and Rei too, at first) regarded Kaworu with suspicion in episode 24, but Shinji was perfectly accepting of this boy who was "like Ayanami". And as Kendrix pointed out, Shinji also thought that Kaworu was a bit like himself. Shinji may not seem as unusual as Rei and Kaworu, but he does know what it feels like to be hurt... to feel like he doesn't belong. (The same can be said for the Rebuild continuity, where the DSS Choker was apparently made by people who feared Kaworu, and by contrast Shinji was very accepting of Kaworu even though he did blatantly strange things like wandering around in dangerous areas without a protective suit!)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Oct 26, 2013 6:24 pm Literary Eagle wrote: He's actually quite kind to people like Rei and Kaworu who are perceived by others as odd or even downright creepy.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Oct 26, 2013 9:06 pm OP, nearly all of this was in EoE. There's enough data on Misato and Asuka alone to fill several well-sized essays. We've got Misato's kiss and Asuka's demented mindtrips through her emotions in relation to Shinji. Now, the question of whether anybody felt the same way about Shinji as Kaworu did would be something harder to answer.

So let us speculate!

Article: Was Kaworu really the only one that had feelings for Shinji
[]: Was Kaworu really the only one that had feelings for Shinji?             Quote Post by Dataprime » Wed Oct 23, 2013 9:03 pm I see Misato as more of a motherly type figure Asuka never really stated her true feelings for him of course they are hints in the show (but you never know) That leaves me with Rei...I don't know what to think about Rei so I'll put her aside for now. So let us speculate!

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Oct 23, 2013 9:07 pm Kaworu didn't actually have any feelings for Shinji. He's a thing. Someone grew him in a lab. He doesn't even identify himself as human. We see how long and how hard Rei has to struggle to come to terms with the human part of her being. There's something decidedly off about Kaworu's demeanor and attitudes. He's a honeypot. He doesn't love Shinji, he barely knows him. All that he represents is an idyllic but totally hollow fantasy that Shinji must crush before he can [s:237xcw70]answer the Riddle of Steel[/s:237xcw70] [s:237xcw70]cross the abyss[/s:237xcw70] experience and reject Instrumentality. No, Shinji, a gorgeous, nonthreatening confidante is not going to drop into your lap and declare undying love without any change, effort or growth on your part.

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Wed Oct 23, 2013 10:14 pm Chuck, you sound almost jelly. That depends on what kind of feelings you're speaking of - Obviously Misato has some motherly affections for Shinji, but if you mean attraction as in the sexual kind, I'm not seeing much there but Tears kind of plays with that a little and so does EoE. Asuka, it's hard to say for me. She seems like she might "like" Shinji because he's: 1) A fellow pilot so one of the few people "worthy" of her affections 2) Does better than her later on in the show and she's jealous 3) She demands attention from everyone, and Shinji wasn't giving her too much of it so she's peeved I would say Rei does have feelings for Shinji, but

Article: Was Kaworu really the only one that had feelings for Shinji
 Does better than her later on in the show and she's jealous 3) She demands attention from everyone, and Shinji wasn't giving her too much of it so she's peeved I would say Rei does have feelings for Shinji, but she's pretty new to this whole "emotion" thing so I don't know if what you would identify as "like" comes into the equation. She might just feel less lonely when she's with Shinji and so she wants to be close with him. How close? I don't know. But Tears makes it pretty clear that he eases her loneliness. Kaworu... I dont even. I have no idea. He's just weird to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Madonna » Wed Oct 23, 2013 10:54 pm Yes. It's never shown nor alluded that angels can't experience feelings anywhere in the anime, Kaworu did have feelings for him; the issue on whether these feelings were of a sexual or platonic nature can definitely be debated and argued however the fact remains that Kaworu thought he loved Shinji and expressed that urge. Even if it was established that angels couldn't experience any feelings whatsoever, Kaworu was presented as being in a human male form, he had an apparent gender; Kaworu is Adam and as Gendo excitedly stated in episode eight Adam was "The first human"; making Kaworu more of a human and angel hybrid than anything else. To call Kaworu's feelings as him just being 'naive' and him 'not knowing what feelings are' is to not give his character and his emotions any importance or credence whatsoever. I also think he was the only one that had any true meaningful feelings for him, not to belittle what Asuka and others may have felt for Shinji, but they weren't really explored so we'll never really know (Although Asuka's felt more like a high school crush than anything).

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Oct 23, 2013 11:15 pm Kaworu is physiologically partially human. It stands to reason that he's capable of emotion. What I question is that whether his experiences as a living being could grant him any emotional development, or whether the time in which he knew Shinji would allow for any depth or genuineness of feeling. On both of those points, I think the answer is "no" and it's more likely that he was throwing around terms like "love"

Article: Was Kaworu really the only one that had feelings for Shinji
 whether the time in which he knew Shinji would allow for any depth or genuineness of feeling. On both of those points, I think the answer is "no" and it's more likely that he was throwing around terms like "love" without knowing what they actually mean. He's vitally important- but as an obstacle, not a friend, confidante, or love interest. Like all of the angels Shinji has to fight, he represents a test. In this case the battle is primarily one of will. Shinji doesn't really kill Kaworu, he kills the part of himself that's capable of accepting this non-threatening being showing up to offer him unconditional love when everyone else is harsh and demanding. I mean in a symbolic sense. He still splats the little bastard.

Misato - genuine love and affection, but she isn't always sure how to show that appropriately. Asuka (original) - wants him to like her, otherwise just (usually) friendly coworkers/rivals. Asuka (DC cut) - she likes him all right, but hates herself for having feelings for such a chump. Rei 2 - will do anything to keep him safe, up to possibly even ignoring an order. Rei 3 - Shinji is the only person who matters, and genuinely wanted to become one being with him. Kaworu - more interested in Shinji as an avatar for mankind than as a person, but would bone. Toji and Kensuke - he's a good dude, always means well. Hikari - If Toji wasn't around she'd probably be one of Shinji's fan club girls. Kaji - part-time dad/cool older bro.

Nope. Rei defied orders to eject from Unit-00, blowing herself up to save Shinji. Rei , defying orders? Enough said. And as for Asuka, I think she had conflicted thoughts about him, but liking him was part of it.

Yes. On a pilosophic/relationsip psychology level, was "true love" where you're together on an equal level, take the other person as they really are and want to be with the other for their own sake? Nope. It's hormones. Miyamura has clearly answered the question to whether Asuka "really likes" Shinji with "No", in case her reactions whenever he was in danger or about to leave the city weren*'t obvious enough, and while I wouldn't take her accussations in EoE completely at face value, her complaints about the honesty of Shinji's own feelings weren't entirely without a few grains of truth - Realistically seen, they were basically being average teenagers, though. So they could be said to "love" each other in the sense that you once "loved" that Ex you're always complaining aboput - the word may very well be appropiate for many contexts, such as the technical/functional/narrative structure ones.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Oct 26, 2013 12:33 pm Nah, you might be getting too close to hamville here. That a good thing isn't permanent, or that you may not be able to keep it, doesn't necessarily have to take away from its inherent level of goodness. It's the unignorable circumstances that create the problem, the surroundings - The point of the exercise is not to shame people for daring to do things that they like and make them feel good. There's only something problematic about it when it occurs at the cost of something. It's not

Article: Was Kaworu really the only one that had feelings for Shinji
 create the problem, the surroundings - The point of the exercise is not to shame people for daring to do things that they like and make them feel good. There's only something problematic about it when it occurs at the cost of something. It's not like Kaworu doesn't profit from Shinji in some ways - he gets his curiosity satisfied, for example, he gets to live new experiences and be special for someone. Shinji himself has this huge inferiority complex towards Kaworu, but that may not necessarily be, and probably isn't what Kaworu thinks or what his ideal outcome/state for the relationship is. Kaworu probably thought he was the one's who's disproportionally fortunate to meet this kindred spirit. We shouldn't get caught up in Shinji's perception just because he's the PoV character - Quite tellingly, he was the one who insisted on lying on the floor when he came to visit Kaworu. He did somewhat recognize this at the end, that they were similar ("Somehow he was like Rei... and like me... I loved him as well. ") but not how far - they were both in a situation where they had to make a huge choice for all of their kind, even thought they were curious and even sympathetic towards the other side, for example. His visit to Rei, at least, does partially indicate that he was deliberately seeking out individuals he percieved as "like him." Like all of us. Most notably, he couldn't self-terminate either. Even Rebuild!Kaworu, who very much can (This one is "older and wiser" and seems to have made his decision a long time ago; Maybe witnessing the suffering and loss of humans after NTI convinced him), appears to percieve the whole Fourth-Impact situation like he's the one who got Shinji into needless trouble through a personal miscalculation (he expected the LoC to be there), instead of the other way around, and merely sees his following sacrifice as cleaning up the mess for Shinji's sake - what this clearly is, however, perhaps more obviously than in the original version, where Kaworu had a theoretical gain from causing TI himself, is a "more hero than thou" moment; One of them had to die, and Kaworu was the one who had the streght to do it, which, in either version, only streghtens Shinji's complex of "unworthyness" and his seeing Kaworu as existing on some sort of pedestral,

Article: Was Kaworu really the only one that had feelings for Shinji
oru was the one who had the streght to do it, which, in either version, only streghtens Shinji's complex of "unworthyness" and his seeing Kaworu as existing on some sort of pedestral, like his bed back in the sleepover scene. it's not like Kaworu (and similar things could be said about Rei - So, Shinji gets her to open up - but not just to him, Rebuild is more pronounced here in that she starts saying hello to people, and has these "Thank you" scenes with Asuka or even Mari, but the original series also had these scenes with her and Touji. but she also pushes him , prompting Touji to muse on his hidden nice guy qualities ) simply takes Shinji as he is... and done with it. He does show him acceptance, but he also talks about Shinji's way of shutting himself off, for example. At least in Kaworu's case, we do have official confirmation that he was also partially meant as "the person who tries to start resolving Shinji's complexes", allthough being the source of one obviously made that harder... it kind of kept Shinji from making this connection that his life, and the rest of the world are now something Kaworu gave him and should be treated as such. That kind of "lone survivor mentality" isn't the solution to everything, Misato's and Kaji's cases make that all too clear, but it may have given him something to go on untill he found something else, so he could do the things that had to be done in the meantime... and there was plenty of shit to do in episode 25'.

[]:             Quote Post by Literary Eagle » Sat Oct 26, 2013 5:06 pm Well said, Kendrix! Shinji and Kaworu's unfortunate circumstances didn't mean that the feelings involved were fake or wrong. I think that dismissing Kaworu's love as a falsehood that had to be destroyed is to unfairly ignore the significance of his sacrifice. Being "a friend loyal unto death" (as described in the "Ode to Joy" lyrics) is an ultimate act of love and friendship... that would be a pretty contradictory way to say "Oh, by the way, this kind of love is fake". I've often seen people dismiss Kaworu and Shinji's interactions as "not real" or "unhealthy" because Shinji allegedly just gets all of Kaworu's love and devotion without

Article: Was Kaworu really the only one that had feelings for Shinji
, by the way, this kind of love is fake". I've often seen people dismiss Kaworu and Shinji's interactions as "not real" or "unhealthy" because Shinji allegedly just gets all of Kaworu's love and devotion without having to do anything in return. But I don't think that's really fair to Shinji. He's actually quite kind to people like Rei and Kaworu who are perceived by others as odd or even downright creepy. Look at how Misato (and Rei too, at first) regarded Kaworu with suspicion in episode 24, but Shinji was perfectly accepting of this boy who was "like Ayanami". And as Kendrix pointed out, Shinji also thought that Kaworu was a bit like himself. Shinji may not seem as unusual as Rei and Kaworu, but he does know what it feels like to be hurt... to feel like he doesn't belong. (The same can be said for the Rebuild continuity, where the DSS Choker was apparently made by people who feared Kaworu, and by contrast Shinji was very accepting of Kaworu even though he did blatantly strange things like wandering around in dangerous areas without a protective suit!)
